paper.title: Runaway origins of a disc mass gradient in $\\sigma$ Orionis

extraction.summary: The paper discusses sigma Ori AB as a runaway system passing through the sigma Orionis cluster. It does not present any objects as possibly unbound from the Milky Way. No HVS or Galactic-unbound candidates are identified.

candidate_groups_considered.description: The paper discusses sigma Ori AB as a runaway system ejected from its birth cluster and currently passing through the sigma Orionis cluster.
candidate_groups_considered.reason: The paper treats sigma Ori AB as a cluster runaway (ejected from one star-forming cluster and passing through another), not as a Galactic-unbound or hypervelocity star candidate. No objects are discussed as possibly escaping the Milky Way gravitational potential.

source_refs.context: Abstract stating that massive stars can be runaways and that sigma Ori AB is a runaway system passing through sigma Orionis
Introduction discussing runaway stars and stating the paper explores sigma Ori AB as a runaway system performing a flyby of sigma Orionis
Section 2.1 concluding that sigma Ori AB is a runaway system passing through the cluster, ejected through dynamical interactions with nearby massive stars
